initial_program:
guard(X) :- color(X).

% rows and columns that contain at least one nonzero cell
used_row(R) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
row(R)       :- used_row(R).

used_col(C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
col(C)       :- used_col(C).

% bounding rectangle of the nonzero cells
minR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #min{X : row(X)}.
maxR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #max{X : row(X)}.
minC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #min{Y : col(Y)}.
maxC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #max{Y : col(Y)}.

% distance of a nonzero cell to the nearest corner
dist(R, C, D) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V),
    (R - minR) <= (maxR - R), Vd = R - minR,
    (C - minC) <= (maxC - C), Hd = C - minC,
    D = max(Vd, Hd).

% colour that appears in the input for a given distance
map_dist(D, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), V != 0, dist(R, C, D), guard(D).

% output cells whose distance has a colour from the input
output(R, C, Col) :- dist(R, C, D), map_dist(D, Col).

% output cells that are zero in the input (stay 0)
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, V), V == 0, guard(V).

% each output cell gets exactly one colour
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output(R, C, Col).

% at most one colour per distance
:- map_dist(D, Col1), map_dist(D, Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
guard(X) :- color(X).

% rows and columns that contain at least one nonzero cell
used_row(R) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
row(R)       :- used_row(R).

used_col(C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
col(C)       :- used_col(C).

% bounding rectangle of the nonzero cells
minR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #min{X : row(X)}.
maxR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #max{X : row(X)}.
minC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #min{Y : col(Y)}.
maxC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #max{Y : col(Y)}.

% distance of a nonzero cell to the nearest corner
dist(R, C, D) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V),
    (R - minR) <= (maxR - R), Vd = R - minR,
    (C - minC) <= (maxC - C), Hd = C - minC,
    D = max(Vd, Hd).

% colour that appears in the input for a given distance
map_dist(D, Col) :- input(R, C, Col), V != 0, dist(R, C, D), guard(D).

% output cells whose distance has a colour from the input
output(R, C, Col) :- dist(R, C, D), map_dist(D, Col).

% output cells that are zero in the input (stay 0)
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, V), V == 0, guard(V).

% each output cell gets exactly one colour
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output(R, C, Col).

% at most one colour per distance
:- map_dist(D, Col1), map_dist(D, Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
guard(X) :- color(X).

% rows and columns that contain at least one nonzero cell
used_row(R) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
row(R)       :- used_row(R).

used_col(C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
col(C)       :- used_col(C).

% bounding rectangle of the nonzero cells
minR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #min{X : row(X)}.
maxR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #max{X : row(X)}.
minC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #min{Y : col(Y)}.
maxC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #max{Y : col(Y)}.

% distance of a nonzero cell to the nearest corner
dist(R, C, D) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V),
    (R - minR) <= (maxR - R), Vd = R - minR,
    (C - minC) <= (maxC - C), Hd = C - minC,
    D = max(Vd, Hd).

% colour that appears in the input for a given distance
map_dist(D,Col) :- input(R, C, V), V = Col, V != 0, dist(R, C, D), guard(D).


% output cells whose distance has a colour from the input
output(R, C, Col) :- dist(R, C, D), map_dist(D, Col).

% output cells that are zero in the input (stay 0)
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, V), V == 0, guard(V).

% each output cell gets exactly one colour
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output(R, C, Col).

% at most one colour per distance
:- map_dist(D, Col1), map_dist(D, Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

#show output/3.

final_program:
guard(X) :- color(X).

% rows and columns that contain at least one nonzero cell
used_row(R) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
row(R)       :- used_row(R).

used_col(C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V).
col(C)       :- used_col(C).

% bounding rectangle of the nonzero cells
minR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #min{X : row(X)}.
maxR(NR) :- row(NR), NR = #max{X : row(X)}.
minC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #min{Y : col(Y)}.
maxC(NC) :- col(NC), NC = #max{Y : col(Y)}.

% distance of a nonzero cell to the nearest corner
dist(R, C, D) :- input(R, C, V), V != 0, guard(V),
    (R - minR) <= (maxR - R), Vd = R - minR,
    (C - minC) <= (maxC - C), Hd = C - minC,
    D = max(Vd, Hd).

% colour that appears in the input for a given distance
map_dist(D,Col) :- input(R, C, V), V = Col, V != 0, dist(R, C, D), guard(D).


% output cells whose distance has a colour from the input
output(R, C, Col) :- dist(R, C, D), map_dist(D, Col).

% output cells that are zero in the input (stay 0)
output(R, C, 0) :- input(R, C, V), V == 0, guard(V).

% each output cell gets exactly one colour
1 { output(R, C, Col) : color(Col) } 1 :- output(R, C, Col).

% at most one colour per distance
:- map_dist(D, Col1), map_dist(D, Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

#show output/3.
